items.title: Community seeks to become sole managers of ACT national park in two years

children.content: The Wreck Bay Aboriginal Community Council  plans to take on sole management of Booderee National Park In May 2028.

image.altText: A group of Indigenous dancers performed at Green Patch

items.title: Domestic violence hotline can only answer 50 per cent of calls

children.content: More than $290 million in federal funding is on the table for domestic violence services nationwide, but Canberra providers are concerned about how it will be distributed and tracked by the ACT government.

image.altText: A person with glasses and a bow tie sits in an office.

items.title: 'Scary': Farmers and fishers face uncertain future as costs soar

children.content: As fuel prices remain high amid the war in the Middle East, growers and fishers selling in Canberra are among those having to make tough decisions to remain viable.

gallery.altText: A fish is held up with tongs.

thumbnail.altText: A fish is held up with tongs.

items.title: 'Epic in scale': APY Lands exhibition opens at NGA after three-year delay

children.content: An exhibition of First Nations art from the APY Lands in South Australia brings together 30 large-scale works by nearly 50 artists.

image.altText: Two woman sitting on while painting.

tags.title: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Art

items.title: Nitrous oxide a 'significant factor' in Canberra teen's drowning death

children.content: An 18-year-old was heavily impaired by nitrous oxide, known as "nangs", when his car entered a pond, prompting a coroner to call for a review of the gas's availability in the ACT.

image.altText: A pond with two road bridges going over it.

items.title: 'Running on the smell of an oily rag': Community fights to open respite centre

children.content: A Queanbeyan family's plan for age-appropriate respite care is nearing completion, but without ongoing funding to secure a provider, the centre may never open its doors.

items.title: Ben Roberts-Smith display at War Memorial altered following arrest

children.content: As the Australian War Memorial unveils some of its newest elements as part of a $500 million redevelopment, director Matt Anderson confirms it has altered a display dedicated to Ben Roberts-Smith in the wake of his arrest for alleged war crimes.

image.altText: The newly altered display on Ben Roberts-Smith

items.title: High Court has made Bruce Lehrmann's defamation case a complete failure

children.content: The High Court was Bruce Lehrmann's last legal avenue to challenge a Federal Court ruling that, on the balance of probabilities, he raped Brittany Higgins — now that avenue has closed.

image.altText: Bruce Lehrman leaves the Federal Court of Australia in Sydney.

items.title: Prince Harry and Meghan kick off Australia visit with stop at Melbourne hospital

children.content: The Duke and Duchess of Sussex have begun their multi-day visit to Australia in Melbourne, meeting with patients at a children's hospital.

articles.title: As the Sussexes return to Australia, 'unusual' commercial engagements are raising eyebrows

children.content: Harry and Meghan will be travelling under their titles of Duke and Duchess of Sussex, and will appear at two major, ticketed events as well as making the rounds of various charities and causes.

image.altText: prince harry and his wife Meghan leaving a cathedral in the UK smiling and happy.

gallery.altText: Meghan Markle talking to a young girl
Prince Harry greets a boy as he visits the Royal Children's Hospital in Melbourne.

thumbnail.altText: Meghan Markle talking to a young girl
Prince Harry greets a boy as he visits the Royal Children's Hospital in Melbourne.

items.title: WA government says it has secured 4 million litres of diesel

children.content: Four million litres of diesel is set to arrive in Western Australia "in the coming weeks" as part of a strategic stockpile to address acute, localised fuel shortages across the state.

image.altText: Fuel prices advertised at more than $2.50 a litre at Ampol petrol station.

items.title: How the AFL's gamble with conflict of interest concerns didn't pay off

children.content: The AFL gambled that concerns over a conflict of interest with one of its umpires wouldn't be an issue, but that call has come back to bite them — and it's not the umpire's fault.

image.altText: An umpire smiles while being interviewed during a press conference.

items.title: 'Make a noise': The ordinary people who took their case to our highest court

children.content: From love and freedom to native title and the right to vote, these are some of the High Court cases that have helped define our nation, and the ordinary people behind them.

gallery.altText: Eddie stands with a group of people, a statue of the scales of justice in the background.
Kim and her two brothers stand holding a sign with their names and ages..

thumbnail.altText: Eddie stands with a group of people, a statue of the scales of justice in the background.
Kim and her two brothers stand holding a sign with their names and ages..
Young man being lead by two police officers

items.title: Trump's 'AI Jesus' post sparks backlash as pope responds to attack

children.content: Donald Trump is criticised by religious sections of his supporter base after publishing, then deleting, the image, but the US president says: "I thought it was me as a doctor."

image.altText: A painting-style image showing Donald Trump in white robes resting his hand on the forehead of a hospital patient.

items.title: Man accused of 'river to the sea' offence tells court he wants to 'plead insanity'

children.content: Jim Dowling is charged with one count of publicly displaying a prohibited expression after displaying a sign reading "from the river to the sea, Brisbane will be free of Boeing."

image.altText: A man with grey hair and beard in a red shirt standing outside court

items.title: Man charged after Molotov cocktail attack on OpenAI CEO's home

children.content: A man accused of throwing a Molotov cocktail at the home of OpenAI's CEO is charged with attempted murder. Authorities say he had written about AI's purported risk to humanity and "our impending extinction".

image.altText: A still of the Molotov cocktail hitting the fence as a suspect turns away.

items.title: Tornado forces Wallaroos to take refuge inside US hotel

children.content: The Wallaroos' preparations for the Pacific Four Series are dealt an unexpected blow as a warning alarm sounds inside the team hotel in Kansas City.

image.altText: Australia sings its national anthem at the Women's Rugby World Cup.

items.title: INXS is out, Phil Collins in as Rock & Roll Hall of Fame 2026 inductees announced

children.content: INXS has missed out on landing a place in the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame with 2026 inductees including Phil Collins, Queen Latifah and Fela Kuti.

gallery.altText: Phil Collins singing into a microphone, mouth wide open, arm up, pink and blue lights in background

thumbnail.altText: Phil Collins singing into a microphone, mouth wide open, arm up, pink and blue lights in background

items.title: How I've learnt to embrace the new normal of only having one breast

children.content: I decided to keep my healthy breast, opting against reconstruction. It means I'm now what's known in the community as a uniboober.

image.altText: Aarthi with her dog during chemo treatment

items.title: Kitty Flanagan and Anne Edmonds create comedy chaos in new show

children.content: Plus Nicole Kidman is an Aussie wrestler in Margo's Got Money Troubles, Baby Reindeer's Richard Gadd returns, and the reboots keep on coming with Malcolm in the Middle.

image.altText: A woman in a red and blue costume leans on the bars of a wrestling rink

items.title: Giant echidna fossil rewrites understanding of Victoria's prehistoric past

children.content: A fossil hidden in a museum collection for 119 years has revealed giant echidnas once roamed Victoria's east, filling a major gap in Australia's prehistoric record.

gallery.altText: an artist's impression of a giant echidna standing in grassland
A photo of a, ancient, brown cranial fossil of an animal
A man in caving equipment standing inside a cave that is illuminated by torch light

thumbnail.altText: an artist's impression of a giant echidna standing in grassland
A photo of a, ancient, brown cranial fossil of an animal
A man in caving equipment standing inside a cave that is illuminated by torch light

items.title: New ministers appointed to Victorian cabinet after resignations

children.content: Four new ministers have been promoted to cabinet unopposed at a meeting of Labor MPs at Victorian parliament this morning.

image.altText: A woman wearing a striped blazer smiles for a professional portrait.

items.title: Prime minister leads tributes after death of NT's first female senator

children.content: A former Arnhem Land schoolteacher who blazed a trail as the Northern Territory’s first female senator, Trish Crossin, has died at age 70.

image.altText: A woman smiles for the camera holding a book and a pen

items.title: Flinders Island man fatally struck by police car consumed '18 handles of beer'

children.content: A coroner dismisses an inquest application from the wife of a Flinders Island man who was fatally struck by a police car after he had a night out at a local pub and left on foot.

image.altText: About one hundred people sat at an outdoor tavern by the water.

items.title: Alleged intruder denies Islamophobic attack at community dinner

children.content: Police say if there is sufficient evidence, a man accused of attacking people at a community dinner could be charged under hate speech laws.

image.altText: A man sits, handcuffed, in a community centre near two police officers.

items.title: Beijing is shifting its Taiwan strategy. Here is why

children.content: It began with a dish once served to Nixon in 1972. Now Beijing is using the same stage to reshape the Taiwan question. China is advancing a slower strategy: using economic incentives and political engagement to influence Taiwan's future.

gallery.altText: A close up of a Chinese man's face

thumbnail.altText: A close up of a Chinese man's face

items.title: Debunking four myths about Australia's fuel crisis

children.content: If you listen to increasingly shrill calls, it is time to rebuild our oil industry. While the sentiments might be well-meant, a few unfortunate facts have been overlooked.

image.altText: Cars queued up for fuel at a Mobil petrol station in Port Moresby

items.title: Roblox to launch accounts for children after grooming claims

children.content: Roblox will launch restricted accounts for children and teenagers, two months after being put on notice by the government over reports children were being groomed on the platform.

image.altText: A boy poses for a photo while holding a smartphone in front of a screen.

items.title: IPL part-ownership possible for Big Bash clubs

children.content: Cricket Australia will know in the coming days if the states are happy to privatise their Big Bash League clubs, a call that may see them become part-owned by Indian Premier League franchises.

image.altText: A group of Perth Scorchers players celebrate on a stage with confetti falling as they hold the Big Bash League men's trophy.

items.title: How this Wests Tigers cult hero went from meme to machine

children.content: For years, Alex Twal was best known for the things he didn't do but, after helping the Tigers surge up the ladder, the cult hero forward has become one of the most improved players in the NRL.

image.altText: A man runs the ball during a rugby league match

items.title: The Australian who allegedly orchestrated Fiji's largest meth importation

children.content: Key insiders from Fiji's largest-ever meth bust say the drug import had alarming links to organised crime in Australia.

items.title: Teenager 'begging' minister to deport abuser who had visa reinstated

children.content: A girl who was sexually assaulted in Perth by a UK citizen when she was nine years old writes to Home Affairs Minister Tony Burke, pleading with him to cancel the man's visa after he avoided deportation on appeal.

image.altText: Tony Burke wears grey suit and is speaking at a press conference.

items.title: Trail mix and water help 87yo woman survive near-freezing night in dense forest

children.content: Police say the woman wandered down a rabbit track after becoming disoriented on a weekend bushwalk.

image.altText: A forest of giant karri trees, looking upwards through the trunks to the canopy

items.title: Sydney summers are now hotter and nearly 50 days longer, study finds

children.content: For every 10 years since 1990, Sydneysiders have experienced an extra 15 days of summer, new research has found.

image.altText: People walk along Opera House forecourt on a hot sunny day

items.title: Starc honoured as Wisden slams England's 'feckless' Ashes

children.content: Australia's Ashes ace Mitchell Starc is named the leading men's cricketer of the year by the Wisden Cricketers' Almanack after taking 55 wickets in 2025.

image.altText: Australian fast bowler Mitchell Starc turns and smiles as he raises his arm in celebration after taking an Ashes wicket.

items.title: The neuroscience behind synaesthesia paints a surprising inner world

children.content: Have you ever tasted a word, or seen colours while listening to music? If you have, you may be among the 1 to 4 per cent of people who have a fascinating trait known as synaesthesia.

gallery.altText: Woman in headphones looks at bright spectrum of lights

thumbnail.altText: Woman in headphones looks at bright spectrum of lights
